zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Stewart於1275年出生在蘇格蘭時，她的父親Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies30歲，母親Lady Margaret de Bonkyl26歲。她於1290年之前與Gillespic Campbell在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1351年7月16日在蘇格蘭金加迪爾享年76歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Stewart于1275年出生在苏格兰时，她的父亲Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies30岁，母亲Lady Margaret de Bonkyl26岁。她于1290年之前与Gillespic Campbell在苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1351年7月16日在苏格兰金加迪爾享年76岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Stewart was born in 1275, in Scotland, her father, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, was 30 and her mother, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, was 26. She married Gillespic Campbell before 1290, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 16 July 1351, in Kincardineshire, Scotland, at the age of 76, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Stewart est née en 1275, à Écosse, son père, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Gillespic Campbell avant 1290, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 juillet 1351, à Kincardineshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Stewart 1275 in Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies 30 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret de Bonkyl 26. Sie hat vor 1290 in Argyll, Scotland Gillespic Campbell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. Juli 1351 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Kincardineshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart è nata il nel 1275 nel Scotland, sua padre, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Gillespic Campbell prima del 1290, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 luglio 1351, nel Kincardineshire, Scotland, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewartが1275年にスコットランドで生まれた時，父Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garliesは30歳，母Lady Margaret de Bonkylは26歳であった。1290年以前にスコットランドアルグイルでGillespic Campbellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1351年7月16日にスコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアで76歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewart(이)가 1275년 스컷랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret de Bonkyl(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1290년 이전 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Gillespic Campbell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1351년 7월 16일 스컷랜드 킨카딘실르에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

mn.Loch Awe, Argyll: Lochawe, Argyle, Шотланд, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart nasceu em 1275, em Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Gillespic Campbell antes 1290, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de julho de 1351, em Kincardineshire, Scotland, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Stewart родилась в 1275 г. в местности Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gillespic Campbell перед 1290 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 июля 1351 г. в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Stewart nació en 1275, en Scotland, su papá, Sir John Stewart, Lord of Bonkyll and Garlies, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Margaret de Bonkyl, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Gillespic Campbell antes de 1290, en Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de julio de 1351, en Kincardineshire, Scotland, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
